title: Agent Control Contract — Exit-Code Table
description: The FROZEN exit-code table of the Agent Control Contract. This artifact is a DATA catalogue (not a JSON-document validator) carrying the code -> {neutral name, const alias, meaning, podFailurePolicy intent} mapping plus mode reachability. agentctl compiles `intent` into Job onExitCodes / podFailurePolicy rules. Versioned by `exit_codes_version` (== surfaces.exit_codes); additive => minor; any change to a code's meaning or to its intent => major. A consumer MUST refuse only an unknown MAJOR; an unrecognised code defaults to intent `retriable` (never a silent FailJob).
x-version-note: The contract pins exit_codes_version to the bare major.minor string "1.0".

intent_vocabulary.description: The CLOSED podFailurePolicy intent vocabulary agentctl compiles into onExitCodes. The intent mapping is total over the table; an unrecognised code defaults to `retriable`.

semantics.complete: Treat as success; no retry, no FailJob (Job counts a completion).
semantics.terminal: Permanent failure of THIS spec; do not retry (config/usage error or a reasoned refusal). Maps to FailJob.
semantics.retriable: Transient/unspecified failure; eligible for backoff retry (Ignore toward backoffLimit).
semantics.policy: Budget/partial outcome; verdict is operator policy (default-retriable but tunable via --budget-exit-code).
semantics.infra: Kernel/infra-set (OOM, ungraceful SIGTERM); infrastructure remediation, not an application retry.

codes.name: EXIT_OK
codes.meaning: Success — one-shot completed / loop clean bound / CLEAN SIGTERM DRAIN (a clean drain exits 0, NOT 143).

codes.name: EXIT_FAILURE
codes.meaning: Generic/unspecified failure; also used for cancelled and crashed runs.

codes.name: EXIT_USAGE
codes.meaning: Config/usage error — validation failed before any side effect. Also covers an invalid --validate-config check and a bad config file.

codes.name: EXIT_PARTIAL
codes.meaning: Partial result emitted (a result-body property, not a status); also used for stalled or loop-detected runs. Remappable via --budget-exit-code.

codes.name: EXIT_INTELLIGENCE
codes.meaning: Intelligence endpoint unreachable / auth error after retries.

codes.name: EXIT_SEMANTIC
codes.meaning: Agent ran correctly but concluded the task cannot be done / refused.

codes.name: EXIT_MCP
codes.meaning: A required MCP server failed connect/handshake or died unrecoverably.

codes.name: EXIT_BUDGET
codes.meaning: Hit max-steps/max-tokens/deadline/tree budget before a result; also used for exhausted-steps, exhausted-tokens, and deadline outcomes. Remappable via --budget-exit-code.

codes.name: EXIT_TIMEOUT
codes.meaning: Hard wall-clock deadline tripped (mnemonic to timeout(1)). Emitted when the supervisor hard-kills a run for exceeding its deadline or getting stuck. A run that reports its own deadline as a budget outcome uses BUDGET(7) instead; see x-mapping-divergence.

codes.name: SIGKILL_EXIT
codes.meaning: 128+SIGKILL — OOM / kubelet hard-kill (raise memory limit). Kernel-set; a conformant agent never returns 137 itself.

codes.name: SIGTERM_EXIT
codes.meaning: 128+SIGTERM — ungraceful exit (drain forced past budget). Kernel-set; DISTINCT from a clean drain, which is 0.

x-mapping-divergence.description: The exit-code TABLE (code -> meaning -> intent) is the wire contract. The status-to-code mapping below is informative only and is NOT part of the frozen contract; only the table is frozen. It records how terminal run statuses map to exit codes, including where that mapping differs from the exit code a status name would suggest.

once_exit.Completed: 0 (EXIT_OK), or 3 (EXIT_PARTIAL) when a usable partial exists
once_exit.ExhaustedSteps | ExhaustedTokens | Deadl: 7 (BUDGET) — a self-reported deadline folds to 7, so 124 is not produced by this status mapping; 124 IS produced by the supervisor kill path, see the code 124 meaning

x-mapping-divergence.status_mapping_prose: Deadline->124, Cancelled->0 (clean drain), Stalled/LoopDetected->3|1. Code 124 is defined and OS-mnemonic but is not produced by the status mapping above.

x-mode-reachability.description: Informational — which codes each mode can produce. Descriptive, not enforced.
x-mode-reachability.note: exit_code=2 (EXIT_USAGE) is never reachable from a run-outcome report (validation fails before any run). 137/143 are kernel-set and never returned by the agent.

x-budget-exit-code-remap.description: An operator may remap EXIT_PARTIAL(3) and EXIT_BUDGET(7) via --budget-exit-code. The default-retriable/policy disposition is operator-tunable, not a fixed verdict.

x-sum-types.exit_disposition: In agent://status the exit_disposition field is null | integer(code) — null until terminal, then the integer code.

x-provisional.p_cost: EXIT_BUDGET(7) is the budget signal; its policy disposition is operator-remappable (see x-budget-exit-code-remap).
x-provisional.code_124_unreachable: The 'unreachable' claim is scoped STRICTLY to the status-to-code mapping, where a self-reported deadline folds into BUDGET(7). 124 IS reachable: the supervisor hard-kills a run that exceeds its deadline or gets stuck, so returned_by_agent=true.
